textPlain: The article... well, it doesn't bury the lede, but it does completely omit it outside of the headline.  For anyone who doesn't know the context: The BBC Micro was built by Acorn Computers, which proceeded to design the Acorn RISC Machine -- later renamed to Advanced RISC Machine and thence to simply "arm".In many ways, the tuple (BBC Micro, Acorn Computers, arm) is analogous to (IBM PC, Intel, x86).

> In many ways, the tuple (BBC Micro, Acorn Computers, arm) is analogous to (IBM PC, Intel, x86)Except the BBC micro didn't use an ARM processor -  it used a 6502. Whereas  the IBM PC did use the Intel processor.
